req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: Mobile Cheque Deposit with Manual Verification

## Project Structure

# conftest.py
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from cheque_deposit import ChequeDepositService, ChequeImage, DepositResult


@pytest.fixture
def mock_image_processor():
    """Mock image processing service to isolate deposit logic."""
    processor = MagicMock()
    processor.validate_image.return_value = True
    processor.extract_amount.return_value = None  # overridden per test
    processor.extract_cheque_number.return_value = "CHQ-001"
    processor.extract_payee.return_value = "John Doe"
    processor.extract_bank_routing.return_value = "021000021"
    return processor


@pytest.fixture
def mock_storage():
    """Mock storage/persistence layer."""
    storage = MagicMock()
    storage.save_deposit.return_value = "DEP-20240101-001"
    return storage


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification_service():
    """Mock notification service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def deposit_service(mock_image_processor, mock_storage, mock_notification_service):
    """Fully wired ChequeDepositService with mocked dependencies."""
    return ChequeDepositService(
        image_processor=mock_image_processor,
        storage=mock_storage,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def valid_cheque_image():
    """A minimal valid ChequeImage object."""
    return ChequeImage(
        front_image=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 100,  # Minimal JPEG header
        back_image=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 100,
        customer_id="CUST-123",
        account_number="ACC-456789",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def high_value_cheque_image():
    """A ChequeImage representing a cheque above the threshold."""
    return ChequeImage(
        front_image=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 100,
        back_image=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 100,
        customer_id="CUST-456",
        account_number="ACC-789012",
    )

## cheque_deposit.py (Interface / Stub for reference)

```python
# cheque_deposit.py
"""
Production implementation stub — replace with real service.
This file defines the interface that tests verify.
"""
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from decimal import Decimal
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional
import uuid


MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD = Decimal("5000.00")


class DepositStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING_VERIFICATION = "PENDING_VERIFICATION"   # amount > $5,000
    APPROVED            = "APPROVED"                # amount <= $5,000, auto-approved
    REJECTED            = "REJECTED"
    FAILED              = "FAILED"


class ImageQualityError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the submitted cheque image is unusable."""


class InvalidChequeError(ValueError):
    """Raised when cheque data cannot be extracted from the image."""


class InvalidAccountError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the target account is invalid or closed."""


class DuplicateChequeError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the same cheque number has already been deposited."""


@dataclass
class ChequeImage:
    front_image:    bytes
    back_image:     bytes
    customer_id:    str
    account_number: str
    mime_type:      str = "image/jpeg"


@dataclass
class DepositResult:
    deposit_id:           str
    status:               DepositStatus
    amount:               Decimal
    requires_manual_review: bool
    cheque_number:        str
    message:              str = ""
    review_ticket_id:     Optional[str] = None


class ChequeDepositService:
    def __init__(self, image_processor, storage, notification_service):
        self.image_processor      = image_processor
        self.storage              = storage
        self.notification_service = notification_service

    def deposit(self, cheque_image: ChequeImage) -> DepositResult:
        # 1. Validate image quality
        if not self.image_processor.validate_image(cheque_image.front_image):
            raise ImageQualityError("Front image failed quality check.")
        if not self.image_processor.validate_image(cheque_image.back_image):
            raise ImageQualityError("Back image failed quality check.")

        # 2. Extract cheque data
        amount = self.image_processor.extract_amount(cheque_image.front_image)
        if amount is None or amount <= Decimal("0"):
            raise InvalidChequeError("Could not extract a valid amount.")

        cheque_number = self.image_processor.extract_cheque_number(
            cheque_image.front_image
        )

        # 3. Determine routing
        requires_manual = amount > MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD
        status = (
            DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION
            if requires_manual
            else DepositStatus.APPROVED
        )

        # 4. Persist & notify
        deposit_id = self.storage.save_deposit(
            customer_id=cheque_image.customer_id,
            account_number=cheque_image.account_number,
            amount=amount,
            status=status,
            cheque_number=cheque_number,
        )

        review_ticket_id = None
        if requires_manual:
            review_ticket_id = self.storage.create_review_ticket(deposit_id)
            self.notification_service.notify_manual_review(
                customer_id=cheque_image.customer_id,
                deposit_id=deposit_id,
                amount=amount,
            )
        else:
            self.notification_service.notify_deposit_received(
                customer_id=cheque_image.customer_id,
                deposit_id=deposit_id,
                amount=amount,
            )

        return DepositResult(
            deposit_id=deposit_id,
            status=status,
            amount=amount,
            requires_manual_review=requires_manual,
            cheque_number=cheque_number,
            review_ticket_id=review_ticket_id,
        )
```

## tests/test_cheque_deposit.py

```python
# tests/test_cheque_deposit.py
"""
Pytest suite for mobile cheque deposit with manual verification.

Requirement:
  - Customers can deposit cheques via mobile image capture.
  - Cheques ABOVE $5,000 shall require manual verification.
  - Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

Threshold rule (explicit):
  amount <= $5,000  → auto-approved   (APPROVED, requires_manual_review=False)
  amount >  $5,000  → manual review   (PENDING_VERIFICATION, requires_manual_review=True)
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, call, patch

from cheque_deposit import (
    ChequeDepositService,
    ChequeImage,
    DepositResult,
    DepositStatus,
    ImageQualityError,
    InvalidChequeError,
    MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD,
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount: Decimal) -> None:
    """Point the mock image processor at a specific dollar amount."""
    mock_image_processor.extract_amount.return_value = amount


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY-PATH — AUTO-APPROVAL (amount <= $5,000)
# ===========================================================================
class TestAutoApprovalBelowThreshold:
    """Cheques at or below $5,000 must be auto-approved without manual review."""

    def test_deposit_well_below_threshold_is_approved(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """A typical low-value cheque ($100) is auto-approved."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("100.00"))

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    def test_deposit_one_cent_is_approved(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """Minimum meaningful amount ($0.01) is auto-approved."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("0.01"))

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    def test_deposit_mid_range_amount_is_approved(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """Mid-range amount ($2,500) is auto-approved."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("2500.00"))

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    # --- Boundary: exact threshold ($5,000.00) ---
    def test_deposit_at_exact_threshold_is_approved(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """
        BOUNDARY TEST — Exact threshold ($5,000.00):
        The requirement says 'above $5,000' triggers manual review,
        therefore $5,000.00 exactly must be auto-approved.
        """
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD)

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED, (
            f"Exact threshold ${MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD} should be APPROVED, "
            f"got {result.status}"
        )
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    def test_deposit_one_cent_below_threshold_is_approved(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """
        BOUNDARY TEST — One cent below threshold ($4,999.99):
        Must be auto-approved (not manually reviewed).
        """
        amount = MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD - Decimal("0.01")
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount)

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    def test_approved_deposit_has_no_review_ticket(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """Auto-approved deposits must not generate a review ticket."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.review_ticket_id is None

    def test_approved_deposit_calls_deposit_notification(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_notification_service,
    ):
        """Auto-approved deposit fires a standard deposit-received notification."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("1000.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_notification_service.notify_deposit_received.assert_called_once()
        mock_notification_service.notify_manual_review.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. MANUAL VERIFICATION (amount > $5,000)
# ===========================================================================
class TestManualVerificationAboveThreshold:
    """Cheques above $5,000 must be routed to manual review."""

    # --- Boundary: one cent above threshold ($5,000.01) ---
    def test_deposit_one_cent_above_threshold_requires_manual_review(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """
        BOUNDARY TEST — One cent above threshold ($5,000.01):
        Must trigger manual verification.
        """
        amount = MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD + Decimal("0.01")
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount)

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION, (
            f"${amount} should be PENDING_VERIFICATION, got {result.status}"
        )
        assert result.requires_manual_review is True

    def test_deposit_well_above_threshold_requires_manual_review(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """A large cheque ($25,000) must be routed to manual review."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("25000.00"))

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION
        assert result.requires_manual_review is True

    def test_deposit_just_above_threshold_creates_review_ticket(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_storage,
    ):
        """A review ticket must be created for every high-value deposit."""
        amount = MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD + Decimal("0.01")
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount)
        mock_storage.create_review_ticket.return_value = "TKT-9001"

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_storage.create_review_ticket.assert_called_once()
        assert result.review_ticket_id == "TKT-9001"

    def test_deposit_above_threshold_sends_manual_review_notification(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_notification_service,
    ):
        """Customer must be notified that their deposit requires manual review."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("10000.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_notification_service.notify_manual_review.assert_called_once()
        mock_notification_service.notify_deposit_received.assert_not_called()

    def test_manual_review_notification_contains_correct_amount(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_notification_service,
    ):
        """Manual-review notification must reference the correct deposit amount."""
        amount = Decimal("7500.00")
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount)

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        _, kwargs = mock_notification_service.notify_manual_review.call_args
        assert kwargs["amount"] == amount

    def test_manual_review_deposit_persisted_with_pending_status(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_storage,
    ):
        """High-value deposit must be saved with PENDING_VERIFICATION status."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("6000.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        _, kwargs = mock_storage.save_deposit.call_args
        assert kwargs["status"] == DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION


# ===========================================================================
# 3. RESULT STRUCTURE & DATA INTEGRITY
# ===========================================================================
class TestDepositResultIntegrity:
    """Verify the DepositResult contains correct, consistent data."""

    def test_result_contains_deposit_id(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor, mock_storage
    ):
        """Every successful deposit must return a unique deposit ID."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("100.00"))
        mock_storage.save_deposit.return_value = "DEP-XYZ-999"

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.deposit_id == "DEP-XYZ-999"
        assert result.deposit_id  # must be non-empty

    def test_result_amount_matches_extracted_amount(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """The result amount must exactly match what was extracted from the image."""
        expected_amount = Decimal("3750.50")
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, expected_amount)

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.amount == expected_amount

    def test_result_cheque_number_populated(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """The cheque number extracted from the image must appear in the result."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("200.00"))
        mock_image_processor.extract_cheque_number.return_value = "CHQ-789456"

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.cheque_number == "CHQ-789456"

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount, expected_status, expected_manual", [
        # Below threshold
        (Decimal("0.01"),    DepositStatus.APPROVED,             False),
        (Decimal("1000.00"), DepositStatus.APPROVED,             False),
        (Decimal("4999.99"), DepositStatus.APPROVED,             False),
        # Exact threshold — auto-approved
        (Decimal("5000.00"), DepositStatus.APPROVED,             False),
        # Above threshold — manual review
        (Decimal("5000.01"), DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION, True),
        (Decimal("5001.00"), DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION, True),
        (Decimal("10000.00"),DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION, True),
        (Decimal("99999.99"),DepositStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION, True),
    ])
    def test_status_matrix(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        amount,
        expected_status,
        expected_manual,
    ):
        """
        Parametrised boundary matrix — verifies correct status and
        manual-review flag for all critical amounts including boundaries.
        """
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, amount)

        result = deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        assert result.status == expected_status, (
            f"Amount ${amount}: expected {expected_status}, got {result.status}"
        )
        assert result.requires_manual_review is expected_manual, (
            f"Amount ${amount}: requires_manual_review should be {expected_manual}"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 4. IMAGE QUALITY VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================
class TestImageQualityValidation:
    """The service must reject unusable or corrupt images before processing."""

    def test_invalid_front_image_raises_image_quality_error(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
    ):
        """A poor-quality front image must raise ImageQualityError."""
        mock_image_processor.validate_image.side_effect = (
            lambda img: img != valid_cheque_image.front_image
        )

        with pytest.raises(ImageQualityError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

    def test_invalid_back_image_raises_image_quality_error(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
    ):
        """A poor-quality back image must raise ImageQualityError."""
        # Front passes, back fails
        call_count = {"n": 0}

        def side_effect(img):
            call_count["n"] += 1
            return call_count["n"] != 2  # Second call (back image) returns False

        mock_image_processor.validate_image.side_effect = side_effect

        with pytest.raises(ImageQualityError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

    def test_empty_front_image_raises_image_quality_error(
        self, deposit_service, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """Empty bytes for front image must raise ImageQualityError."""
        mock_image_processor.validate_image.return_value = False

        bad_image = ChequeImage(
            front_image=b"",
            back_image=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 100,
            customer_id="CUST-123",
            account_number="ACC-456789",
        )

        with pytest.raises(ImageQualityError):
            deposit_service.deposit(bad_image)

    def test_rejected_image_does_not_persist_deposit(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_storage,
    ):
        """No deposit record must be created when image validation fails."""
        mock_image_processor.validate_image.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(ImageQualityError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_storage.save_deposit.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. INVALID CHEQUE DATA
# ===========================================================================
class TestInvalidChequeData:
    """The service must reject cheques whose data cannot be reliably extracted."""

    def test_none_amount_raises_invalid_cheque_error(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """If the amount cannot be read from the image, raise InvalidChequeError."""
        mock_image_processor.extract_amount.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(InvalidChequeError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

    def test_zero_amount_raises_invalid_cheque_error(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """A zero-dollar cheque is not a valid deposit."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("0.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InvalidChequeError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

    def test_negative_amount_raises_invalid_cheque_error(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor
    ):
        """A negative amount (e.g. reversed OCR) is not valid."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("-50.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InvalidChequeError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

    def test_invalid_amount_does_not_create_review_ticket(
        self,
        deposit_service,
        valid_cheque_image,
        mock_image_processor,
        mock_storage,
    ):
        """No review ticket must be created when the cheque data is invalid."""
        mock_image_processor.extract_amount.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(InvalidChequeError):
            deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_storage.create_review_ticket.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 6. STORAGE & PERSISTENCE
# ===========================================================================
class TestStoragePersistence:
    """All successful deposits must be correctly persisted."""

    def test_save_deposit_called_with_customer_id(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor, mock_storage
    ):
        """The correct customer ID must be forwarded to storage."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("500.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        _, kwargs = mock_storage.save_deposit.call_args
        assert kwargs["customer_id"] == valid_cheque_image.customer_id

    def test_save_deposit_called_with_account_number(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor, mock_storage
    ):
        """The correct account number must be forwarded to storage."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("500.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        _, kwargs = mock_storage.save_deposit.call_args
        assert kwargs["account_number"] == valid_cheque_image.account_number

    def test_save_deposit_called_exactly_once(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor, mock_storage
    ):
        """Storage must be written to exactly once per deposit call."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("1000.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        mock_storage.save_deposit.assert_called_once()

    def test_approved_deposit_saved_with_approved_status(
        self, deposit_service, valid_cheque_image, mock_image_processor, mock_storage
    ):
        """Auto-approved deposits must be persisted with APPROVED status."""
        configure_amount(mock_image_processor, Decimal("500.00"))

        deposit_service.deposit(valid_cheque_image)

        _, kwargs = mock_storage.save_deposit.call_args
        assert kwargs["status"] == DepositStatus.APPROVED


# ===========================================================================
# 7. THRESHOLD CONSTANT INTEGRITY
# ===========================================================================
class TestThresholdConstant:
    """Sanity-check the threshold constant itself so tests are self-consistent."""

    def test_threshold_is_five_thousand(self):
        """The manual-review threshold must be exactly $5,000.00."""
        assert MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD == Decimal("5000.00"), (
            "Threshold changed — review all boundary tests."
        )

    def test_threshold_is_decimal_type(self):
        """Threshold must be Decimal to avoid floating-point comparison errors."""
        assert isinstance(MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD, Decimal)

    def test_one_cent_above_threshold_exceeds_threshold(self):
        """Arithmetic sanity: $5,000.01 > $5,000.00."""
        above = MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD + Decimal("0.01")
        assert above > MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD

    def test_exact_threshold_does_not_exceed_threshold(self):
        """Arithmetic sanity: $5,000.00 is NOT above $5,000.00."""
        assert not (MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD > MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD)
```

## Running the Tests

```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install pytest pytest-mock

# Run all tests with verbose output
pytest tests/test_cheque_deposit.py -v

# Run only boundary tests
pytest tests/test_cheque_deposit.py -v -k "boundary or threshold or exact"

# Run with coverage
pytest tests/test_cheque_deposit.py --cov=cheque_deposit --cov-report=term-missing
```

## Coverage Map

| Test Class | Requirement Coverage |
|---|---|
| `TestAutoApprovalBelowThreshold` | Auto-approve ≤ $5,000; exact + below boundaries |
| `TestManualVerificationAboveThreshold` | Manual review > $5,000; exact above boundary; ticket & notification |
| `TestDepositResultIntegrity` | Correct result data; parametrised boundary matrix |
| `TestImageQualityValidation` | Mobile image quality gate; no side-effects on failure |
| `TestInvalidChequeData` | Bad OCR / zero / negative amounts rejected cleanly |
| `TestStoragePersistence` | Correct data forwarded to persistence layer |
| `TestThresholdConstant` | Threshold value & type guards; arithmetic sanity |

## cheque_deposit.py (Interface / Stub for reference)

```python
# cheque_deposit.py
"""
Production implementation stub — replace with real service.
This file defines the interface that tests verify.
"""
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from decimal import Decimal
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional
import uuid


MANUAL_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD = Decimal("5000.00")


class DepositStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING_VERIFICATION = "PENDING_VERIFICATION"   # amount > $5,000
    APPROVED            = "APPROVED"                # amount <= $5,000, auto-approved
    REJECTED            = "REJECTED"
    FAILED              = "FAILED"


class ImageQualityError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the submitted cheque image is unusable."""


class InvalidChequeError(ValueError):
    """Raised when cheque data cannot be extracted from the image."""


class InvalidAccountError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the target account is invalid or closed."""


class DuplicateChequeError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the same cheque number has already been deposited."""


@dataclass
class ChequeImage:
    front_image:    bytes
    back_image:     bytes
    customer_id:    str
    account_number: str
    mime_type:      str = "image/jpeg"


@dataclass
class DepositResult:
    deposit_id:           str
    status:               DepositStatus
    amount:               Decimal
    requires_manual_review: bool
    cheque_number:        str
    message:              str = ""
    review_ticket_id:     Optional[str] = None


class ChequeDepositService:
    def __init__(self, image_processor, storage, notification_service):
        self.image_processor      = image_processor
        self.storage              = storage
        self.notification_service = notification_service

    def deposit(self, cheque_image: ChequeImage) -> DepositResul
